items.title: Genk veroordeelt wangedrag supporters en vergoedt kosten gedupeerde fans
items.summary: RC Genk heeft het gedrag van een groep supporters voorafgaan aan het Europa League-duel met FC Utrecht in een statement veroordeeld. Door problemen bij de toegangscontrole, begon het duel donderdagavond bijna een uur later .

items.title: Lang landt in Istanbul voor medische keuring bij Galatasaray
items.summary: Noa Lang staat op het punt zijn transfer naar Galatasaray af te ronden. De 26-jarige linksbuiten, die zal worden gehuurd van Napoli, landde donderdagavond met een privéjet in Istanbul.

items.title: Fingal’s Hill looks to complete the hat-trick at Doncaster
items.summary: We have a busy day’s racing on Friday with action from Doncaster, Southwell, Wolverhampton and Meydan, live on Sky Sports Racing...

items.title: 'He's my son' - Emery on pushing Tielemans
items.summary: Aston Villa manager Unai Emery plays down a heated touchline incident with midfielder Youri Tielemans after substituting him against Fenerbahce.

items.title: 'Bayern aast op RB Leipzig-sensatie, Saoedi's dromen van superster'
items.summary: De tijd van winterse versterkingen is aangebroken, in dit blog volgen we alle transferactiviteit in het buitenland. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: Resilience in Bologna shows O’Neill has transformed Celtic
items.summary: Martin O’Neill’s winning run on his latest return came to an end away to Bologna but no Celtic fan will be feeling dismayed after their backs-to-the-wall 2-2 draw in Italy. This was the best of the lot, a performance that said plenty about the team’s spirit.

items.title: Highlights: Scheffler man to beat, MacIntyre also one off lead at American Express
items.summary: Highlights from day one of the The American Express.

items.title: Jans woedend: 'Dit is zeer onrechtvaardig'
items.summary: Ron Jans baalde na het verlies van FC Utrecht tegen RC Genk van de beslissing van de arbitrage om de 0-1 van Zakaria El Ouahdi goed te keuren.

items.title: Koers nei Europa: clubiconen beoordelen het ambitieuze SC Heerenveen
items.summary: SC Heerenveen heeft de ambitie om Europees voetbal te halen, maar hoe goed is de ploeg van Robin Veldman nu eigenlijk precies? Clubcoryfeeën Riemer van der Velde (85), Foppe de Haan (82) en Gertjan Verbeek (63) erkennen de stijgende lijn, maar sluiten nog niet aan in de polonaise. ‘Het is nou niet zo dat je bang hoeft te zijn dat er in de zomer een half elftal wordt weggekocht, hè?’

items.title: Rodman contract saga ends with record Spirit deal
items.summary: United States forward Trinity Rodman signed a record three-year contract with the Washington Spirit on Thursday to remain at the club, ending months-long uncertainty over the star's future.

items.title: Casemiro to leave Man United at end of the season
items.summary: Casemiro will leave Manchester United when his contract expires at the end of the season, the club have confirmed.

items.title: Dual-nationals help Mexico snap winless streak
items.summary: Dual-nationals Richard Ledezma and Brian Gutierrez earned starts on Thursday as Mexico claimed a victory over Panama.

items.title: Dzeko, 39, returns to Germany with eye on WC
items.summary: Edin Dzeko has returned to Germany where he hopes to play more to help Bosnia and Herzegovina earn a World Cup ticket.

items.title: From write-off to right on track - what is next for Rohl's Rangers?
items.summary: BBC Sport Scotland examines Rangers' turn in fortunes recently under Danny Rohl.

items.title: Celtic show 'spirit' & 'resilience' in Bologna as big week looms
items.summary: Martin O'Neill praised his team for claiming a draw in Italy, but big tests are still to come.

items.title: Premier League: 10 things to look out for this weekend
items.summary: Mateus Fernandes steels West Ham, Dominic Calvert-Lewin faces a homecoming and Manchester City need Marc Guéhi Top scorers: check out the latest standings The absence of the wantaway Lucas Paquetá has given Mateus Fernandes a chance to take on more responsibility for West Ham. Paquetá, who is said to be nursing a minor back problem, was unavailable again for last week’s win against Spurs but Nuno Espírito Santo’s struggling side coped without the Flamengo target. They called on Fernandes to dictate the flow in midfield and the diligent Portuguese did not disappoint. Fernandes moved the ball cleverly, picked up an assist and looked like that rarest of things: a smart signing from West Ham. They will need the 21-year-old, who joined from Southampton for £38m last summer, to shine again with Paquetá looking unlikely to return against high-flying Sunderland at the London Stadium. Jacob Steinberg West Ham v Sunderland, Saturday 12.30pm (all times GMT) Burnley v Tottenham, Saturday 3pm Fulham v Brighton, Saturday 3pm Manchester City v Wolves, Saturday 3pm Bournemouth v Liverpool, Saturday 5.30pm Continue reading...

items.title: How 55 seconds left Forest's Europa League hopes in balance
items.summary: Nottingham Forest's hopes of a top-eight finish in the Europa League - and a straight route to the last 16 - are hanging by a thread after 55 costly seconds lead to defeat by Sporting Braga.

items.title: Europa League roundup: Tielemans and Emery clash as Sancho seals Villa progress
items.summary: Manager shoves his own midfielder in stoppage time Aston Villa into last 16 with victory at Fenerbahce Aston Villa sealed a top-eight finish in the Europa League after Jadon Sancho’s first goal for the club gave them a 1-0 win over Fenerbahce in Turkey. But the Villa manager, Unai Emery, was involved in a touchline spat with Youri Tielemans after the midfielder was substituted in stoppage time. Villa’s win was their sixth from seven European matches this season and ended Fenerbahce’s unbeaten home record. Sancho opened his account in Villa colours to put the visitors in the driving seat amid a loud Sukru Saracoglu Stadium atmosphere. Continue reading...

items.title: Ravens hire Chargers defensive coordinator Minter as head coach
items.summary: Baltimore Ravens have appointed Jesse Minter as their new head coach after John Harbaugh was sacked after 18 years earlier this month.

items.title: Gibbs-White fails from the spot as Nottingham Forest pay for errors in Braga
items.summary: Nottingham Forest’s first European adventure for almost 30 years was supposed to be a hoot but the mood music that accompanied a slender defeat in Braga felt rather alarming. The fact is Forest failed to perform and a stale display was typified by the chain of errors that culminated in the captain, Ryan Yates, scoring an own goal that proved sufficient to earn the hosts victory. A swell of the 2,100 away fans who made the trip to northern Portugal relayed their feelings to Sean Dyche and his squad, jeering the players on several occasions. At full time the question on supporters’ lips was a slightly more direct version of: what on earth was that? Continue reading...
